papers.title: Evaluation of Audio Language Models for Fairness, Safety, and Security
papers.titleJa: オーディオ言語モデルの公平性、安全性、セキュリティの評価
papers.what: オーディオ大規模言語モデル（ALLM）の公平性、安全性、セキュリティ（FSS）を評価するための統一的なフレームワークを提案。
papers.novel: ALLMの構造的分類を導入し、音声入力表現と意味的推論の位置に基づく評価を可能にする新しい視点を提供。
papers.method: 音声入力の表現形式（離散/連続）と意味的推論の位置（カスケード/マルチモーダル/音声ネイティブ）に基づく分類と評価フレームワークを構築。
papers.validation: 2つの代表的なALLMシステムを対象に、拒否率、攻撃成功率、毒性の違いを評価し、モデル設計とFSS挙動の関連性を実証。
papers.discussion: FSS評価のための構造認識アプローチの重要性を示したが、さらなるモデル間の比較と評価基準の精緻化が必要。

nextReads.label: Fairness and Bias in Language Models (2021)
Robustness of Speech Models to Adversarial Attacks (2022)

papers.abstract: Audio large language models (ALLMs) have recently advanced spoken interaction by integrating speech processing with large language models. However, existing evaluations of fairness, safety, and security (FSS) remain fragmented, largely because ALLMs differ fundamentally in how acoustic information is represented and where semantic reasoning occurs. Differences that are rarely made explicit. As a result, evaluations often conflate structurally distinct systems, obscuring the relationship between model design and observed FSS behavior. In this work, we introduce a structural taxonomy (system-level and representational) of ALLMs that categorizes systems along two axes: the form of audio input representation (e.g., discrete vs. continuous) and the locus of semantic reasoning (e.g., cascaded, multimodal, or audio-native). Building on the taxonomy, we propose a unified evaluation framework that assesses semantic invariance under paralinguistic variation, refusal and toxicity behavior under unsafe prompts, and robustness to adversarial audio perturbations. We apply this framework to two representative systems and observe systematic differences in refusal rates, attack success, and toxicity between audio and text inputs. Our findings demonstrate that FSS behavior is tightly coupled to how acoustic information is integrated into semantic reasoning, underscoring the need for structure-aware evaluation of audio language models.

papers.title: SGPA: Spectrogram-Guided Phonetic Alignment for Feasible Shapley Value Explanations in Multimodal Large Language Models
papers.titleJa: SGPA: マルチモーダル大規模言語モデルのためのスペクトログラム誘導音素アライメントによるシャープレイ値説明の実現可能性
papers.what: 音声言語モデルの振る舞いをシャープレイ値帰属を用いて説明するための新しい手法SGPAを提案。
papers.novel: 音声データのアライメントを改善することで、シャープレイ値計算の効率を43倍向上させ、音声モデルの説明可能性を実現。
papers.method: CTC強制アライメントとスペクトル境界の精緻化を組み合わせた4段階のパイプラインを構築し、音響的に安定した単語アライメントを生成。
papers.validation: LFM2-Audio-1.5BモデルとVoiceBenchデータセットを用いた診断で、帰属集中度の変化を確認しつつ、グローバルな累積プロファイルを維持。
papers.discussion: SGPAは説明可能性を向上させるが、他のモデルやデータセットでのさらなる検証が必要。

nextReads.label: Explaining Neural Networks with Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (2015)
LFM2-Audio: A Benchmark for Audio Models (2022)

papers.abstract: Explaining the behavior of end-to-end audio language models via Shapley value attribution is intractable under native tokenization: a typical utterance yields over $150$ encoder frames, inflating the coalition space by roughly $10^{42}$ relative to text; individual audio frames lack standalone meaning; and token boundaries that bisect phonetic transitions introduce masking artifacts. We introduce Spectrogram-Guided Phonetic Alignment (SGPA), a four-stage pipeline that combines Connectionist Temporal Classification forced alignment with spectral boundary refinement to produce acoustically stable, word-aligned audio segments. Controlled diagnostics on LFM2-Audio-1.5B with VoiceBench show that SGPA yields a 43$\times$ reduction in model evaluations. Statistical testing confirms that SGPA significantly alters attribution concentration while preserving the global cumulative profile, establishing it as a feasibility-enabling layer for audio explainability.
papers.comment: Submitted for admission in Interspeech 2026 conference

papers.title: UniWhisper: Efficient Continual Multi-task Training for Robust Universal Audio Representation
papers.titleJa: UniWhisper: 頑健なユニバーサル音声表現のための効率的な継続的マルチタスク学習
papers.what: 音声、環境音楽、音楽を単一のエンコーダで処理可能なユニバーサル音声表現を目指した継続的マルチタスク学習フレームワークUniWhisperを提案。
papers.novel: 異なる音声タスクを統一的な命令と応答形式にキャストすることで、タスク固有のヘッドや損失関数を必要とせずに学習可能。
papers.method: 38,000時間の公開音声データを用いて、標準的な次トークン学習を実施。浅いMLPプローブやkNNを使用して性能を評価。
papers.validation: 20の音声、環境音、音楽タスクで評価を行い、MLPプローブで0.81、kNNで0.61の正規化加重平均を達成。
papers.discussion: 音声性能を維持しつつ他のタスクでの性能を向上させたが、さらなるタスクやデータセットでの評価が必要。

nextReads.label: Whisper: Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision (2022)
Wav2Vec 2.0: A Framework for Self-Supervised Learning of Speech Representations (2020)

papers.abstract: A universal audio representation should capture fine-grained speech cues and high-level semantics for environmental sounds and music in a single encoder. Existing encoders often excel in one domain but degrade in others. We propose UniWhisper, an efficient continual multi-task training framework that casts heterogeneous audio tasks into a unified instruction and answer format. This enables standard next-token training without task-specific heads and losses. We train it on 38k hours of public audio and assess the encoder using shallow MLP probes and k-nearest neighbors (kNN) on 20 tasks spanning speech, environmental sound, and music. UniWhisper reaches normalized weighted averages of 0.81 with MLP probes and 0.61 with kNN, compared to 0.64 and 0.46 for Whisper, while retaining strong speech performance.

papers.title: ReHear: Iterative Pseudo-Label Refinement for Semi-Supervised Speech Recognition via Audio Large Language Models
papers.titleJa: ReHear: 音声大規模言語モデルを用いた半教師あり音声認識のための反復的擬似ラベル改良
papers.what: 音声大規模言語モデルを活用し、半教師あり音声認識における擬似ラベルの精度を反復的に改善するReHearフレームワークを提案。
papers.novel: ASR仮説と元の音声データの両方を条件として使用することで、従来のテキストベースの補正手法よりも高精度な擬似ラベル生成を実現。
papers.method: 音声対応の大規模言語モデルを自己学習ループに統合し、音声とASR仮説を条件とした擬似ラベルの改良を反復的に行う。
papers.validation: 多様なベンチマークでの実験により、エラー伝播を軽減し、従来の教師ありおよび擬似ラベルベースラインを一貫して上回る性能を実証。
papers.discussion: 音声大規模言語モデルの計算コストや、他の言語やドメインへの適用可能性が課題。
papers.abstract: Semi-supervised learning in automatic speech recognition (ASR) typically relies on pseudo-labeling, which often suffers from confirmation bias and error accumulation due to noisy supervision. To address this limitation, we propose ReHear, a framework for iterative pseudo-label refinement that integrates an instruction-tuned, audio-aware large language model (LLM) into the self-training loop. Unlike conventional text-based correctors, our approach conditions the LLM on both the ASR hypothesis and the source audio, allowing it to recover phonetically accurate transcripts even from severe recognition errors. These refined pseudo-labels serve as high-fidelity targets for fine-tuning the ASR model in an iterative cycle. Experimental results across diverse benchmarks demonstrate that ReHear effectively mitigates error propagation, consistently outperforming both supervised and pseudo-labeling baselines.

papers.title: Scaling Audio-Text Retrieval with Multimodal Large Language Models
papers.titleJa: マルチモーダル大規模言語モデルを用いた音声-テキスト検索のスケーリング
papers.what: 音声信号と自然言語を結びつける音声-テキスト検索のために、マルチモーダル大規模言語モデル（MLLM）を活用した新しいフレームワークAuroLAを提案。
papers.novel: MLLMを統一バックボーンとして活用し、多粒度のテキストキャプション生成やハイブリッドNCE損失を導入することで、少量のデータで最先端モデルを上回る性能を実現。
papers.method: 多様な音声データを収集し、自動アノテーションで多粒度のキャプションを生成。MLLMを適応させ、音声とテキストの埋め込みを生成し、ハイブリッドNCE損失で学習を行う。
papers.validation: PE-AVを含む最先端モデルと比較し、約1%のデータ量で一貫して優れた性能を示すことを実験的に確認。
papers.discussion: MLLMのさらなるスケーリングや、異なるドメインでの性能検証が必要。
papers.abstract: Audio-text retrieval is crucial for bridging acoustic signals and natural language. While contrastive dual-encoder architectures like CLAP have shown promise, they are fundamentally limited by the capacity of small-scale encoders. Specifically, the text encoders struggle to understand complex queries that require reasoning or world knowledge. In this paper, we propose AuroLA, a novel contrastive language-audio pre-training framework that re-purposes Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) as a unified backbone for retrieval. Specifically, we make three contributions: (i) we construct a scalable data pipeline that curates diverse audio from multiple sources and generates multi-granular captions, ranging from long descriptions to structured tags, via automated annotation; (ii) we adapt an MLLM for retrieval by prompting it to summarize the audio/text input and using the hidden state of a special token as audio/text embeddings. For model training, we devise a novel Hybrid-NCE loss, which employs multi-granular supervision and hard-negative reweighting to robustly align audio with diverse textual supervision; and (iii) we design an MLLM-based bidirectional re-ranking module that refines retrieval candidates through deep cross-modal interaction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AuroLA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art models, including the recent PE-AV, while utilizing only approximately 1% of PE-AV's training data. Lastly, we observe clear scaling trends regarding dataset size and model capacity, validating the effectiveness of MLLM as a unified backbone for audio-text retrieval. Code is available at https://github.com/Jazzcharles/AuroLA.

papers.title: Moving Speaker Separation via Parallel Spectral-Spatial Processing
papers.titleJa: 移動話者分離のための並列スペクトル・空間処理
papers.what: 動的環境下でのマルチチャネル音源分離のために、スペクトル特徴と空間特徴を並列に処理する新しいアーキテクチャPS2を提案。
papers.novel: スペクトルと空間の特徴を並列に処理することで、従来のシーケンシャルアプローチのモデリングの衝突を解消し、移動話者シナリオでの性能を大幅に向上。
papers.method: スペクトル分岐にはBLSTMベースの周波数モジュール、Mambaベースの時間モジュール、自己注意モジュールを使用し、空間分岐にはBGRUネットワークを採用。クロスアテンション融合機構で両分岐の特徴を統合。
papers.validation: WHAMR!やWSJ0-Demand-6ch-Moveデータセットを用い、SI-SDRで1.6-2.2 dBの改善を達成。高速な話者移動でも13 dB以上の改善を維持。
papers.discussion: 高速移動や異なる残響時間、ノイズレベルに対しても頑健性を示すが、さらなる実環境での評価が必要。

nextReads.label: Deep Clustering and Conventional Networks for Music Separation (2017)
Conv-TasNet: Surpassing Ideal Time-Frequency Magnitude Masking for Speech Separation (2018)
Self-Attentive Spectral-Spatial Features for Speech Separation (2021)

papers.abstract: Multi-channel speech separation in dynamic environments is challenging as time-varying spatial and spectral features evolve at different temporal scales. Existing methods typically employ sequential architectures, forcing a single network stream to simultaneously model both feature types, creating an inherent modeling conflict. In this paper, we propose a dual-branch parallel spectral-spatial (PS2) architecture that separately processes spectral and spatial features through parallel streams. The spectral branch uses a bi-directional long short-term memory (BLSTM)-based frequency module, a Mamba-based temporal module, and a self-attention module to model spectral features. The spatial branch employs bi-directional gated recurrent unit (BGRU) networks to process spatial features that encode the evolving geometric relationships between sources and microphones. Features from both branches are integrated through a cross-attention fusion mechanism that adaptively weights their contributions. Experimental results demonstrate that the PS2 outperforms existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods by 1.6-2.2 dB in scale-invariant signal-to-distortion ratio (SI-SDR) for moving speaker scenarios, with robust separation quality under different reverberation times (RT60), noise levels, and source movement speeds. Even with fast source movements, the proposed model maintains SI-SDR improvements of over 13 dB. These improvements are consistently observed across multiple datasets, including WHAMR! and our generated WSJ0-Demand-6ch-Move dataset.
papers.comment: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing

papers.title: Robust Long-Form Bangla Speech Processing: Automatic Speech Recognition and Speaker Diarization
papers.titleJa: ロバストな長時間バングラ音声処理: 自動音声認識と話者分離
papers.what: バングラ語の長時間音声認識（ASR）と話者分離のためのエンドツーエンドシステムを提案。
papers.novel: 低リソース言語であるバングラ語に特化した微調整モデルを使用し、ASRと話者分離の両方で高い性能を達成。
papers.method: BengaliAIで微調整したWhisperモデルを使用し、Demucsによる音源分離、沈黙境界のチャンク化、pyannote.audioのセグメンテーションモデルのカスタマイズを実施。
papers.validation: DL Sprint 4.0コンペティションで、ASRのWERが0.36137、話者分離のDERが0.20936を達成。
papers.discussion: 低リソース言語での課題を克服するため、さらなるデータ収集とモデルの改良が必要。

nextReads.label: Whisper: Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision (2022)
Demucs: Deep Extractor for Music Sources (2020)
pyannote.audio: Neural Building Blocks for Speaker Diarization (2020)

papers.abstract: We describe our end-to-end system for Bengali long-form speech recognition (ASR) and speaker diarization submitted to the DL Sprint 4.0 competition on Kaggle. Bengali presents substantial challenges for both tasks: a large phoneme inventory, significant dialectal variation, frequent code-mixing with English, and a relative scarcity of large-scale labelled corpora. For ASR we achieve a best private Word Error Rate (WER) of 0.37738 and public WER of 0.36137, combining a BengaliAI fine-tuned Whisper medium model with Demucs source separation for vocal isolation, silence-boundary chunking, and carefully tuned generation hyperparameters. For speaker diarization we reach a best private Diarization Error Rate (DER) of 0.27671 and public DER of 0.20936 by replacing the default segmentation model inside the pyannote.audio pipeline with a Bengali-fine-tuned variant, pairing it with wespeaker-voxceleb-resnet34-LM embeddings and centroid-based agglomerative clustering. Our experiments demonstrate that domain-specific fine-tuning of the segmentation component, vocal source separation, and natural silence-aware chunking are the three most impactful design choices for low-resource Bengali speech processing.
papers.comment: 6 pages, 5 figures, 3 tables; system paper submitted to DL Sprint 4.0 (Kaggle)

papers.title: A Knowledge-Driven Approach to Music Segmentation, Music Source Separation and Cinematic Audio Source Separation
papers.titleJa: 音楽セグメンテーション、音楽ソース分離、および映画音響ソース分離のための知識駆動型アプローチ
papers.what: 音楽スコアなどの知識を活用し、事前にセグメント化されたデータに依存せずに音声を単一カテゴリまたは混合カテゴリのチャンクに分割し、ソース分離を行うモデルベースのアプローチを提案。
papers.novel: 従来のデータ駆動型手法と異なり、事前にセグメント化されたデータに依存せず、入力音声と関連知識から自律的にモデルを構築する点が新規性。
papers.method: 隠れマルコフモデルなどのモデルを用いて、音声セグメンテーションと認識を行い、知識駆動型の学習でスコアに基づく音楽セグメンテーションと分離を実現。
papers.validation: シミュレーションデータでの評価により、スコアガイド型学習が高い精度の音楽セグメンテーションと分離を達成し、映画音響データでは従来のデータ駆動型手法を上回る結果を示した。
papers.discussion: 知識駆動型アプローチの適用範囲や、異なる種類の音声データに対する一般化性能が今後の課題。

nextReads.label: Deep Learning for Music Source Separation (2021)
Hidden Markov Models for Audio Processing (2018)

papers.abstract: We propose a knowledge-driven, model-based approach to segmenting audio into single-category and mixed-category chunks with applications to source separation. "Knowledge" here denotes information associated with the data, such as music scores. "Model" here refers to tool that can be used for audio segmentation and recognition, such as hidden Markov models. In contrast to conventional learning that often relies on annotated data with given segment categories and their corresponding boundaries to guide the learning process, the proposed framework does not depend on any pre-segmented training data and learns directly from the input audio and its related knowledge sources to build all necessary models autonomously. Evaluation on simulation data shows that score-guided learning achieves very good music segmentation and separation results. Tested on movie track data for cinematic audio source separation also shows that utilizing sound category knowledge achieves better separation results than those obtained with data-driven techniques without using such information.

papers.title: 823-OLT @ BUET DL Sprint 4.0: Context-Aware Windowing for ASR and Fine-Tuned Speaker Diarization in Bengali Long Form Audio
papers.titleJa: 823-OLT @ BUET DL Sprint 4.0: ベンガル語長尺音声のASRと話者分離のための文脈認識型ウィンドウ処理
papers.org: Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET)
papers.what: ベンガル語の長尺音声における自動音声認識（ASR）と話者分離のためのフレームワークを提案し、文脈を考慮したウィンドウ処理と事前学習済みモデルのファインチューニングを活用。
papers.novel: 低リソース言語であるベンガル語に特化したASRと話者分離技術を提供し、文脈認識型ウィンドウ処理やファインチューニングにより精度を向上させた点が新規性。
papers.method: Whisper MediumモデルをベースにしたASRパイプラインを構築し、音源分離、音声活動検出、ギャップ認識型ウィンドウ処理を組み合わせて安定したデコードを実現。話者分離には、公式競技データセットを用いて事前学習済みモデルをファインチューニング。
papers.validation: BUET CSE FestのDL Sprint 4.0競技データセットを用いて評価し、長尺音声の効率的な文字起こしと話者認識の精度向上を実証。
papers.discussion: 提案手法の他言語への適用可能性や、さらに多様なデータセットでの評価が今後の課題。
papers.abstract: Bengali, despite being one of the most widely spoken languages globally, remains underrepresented in long form speech technology, particularly in systems addressing transcription and speaker attribution. We present frameworks for long form Bengali speech intelligence that address automatic speech recognition using a Whisper Medium based model and speaker diarization using a finetuned segmentation model. The ASR pipeline incorporates vocal separation, voice activity detection, and a gap aware windowing strategy to construct context preserving segments for stable decoding. For diarization, a pretrained speaker segmentation model is finetuned on the official competition dataset (provided as part of the DL Sprint 4.0 competition organized under BUET CSE Fest), to better capture Bengali conversational patterns. The resulting systems deliver both efficient transcription of long form audio and speaker aware transcription to provide scalable speech technology solutions for low resource languages.

papers.title: Mind the Gap: Detecting Cluster Exits for Robust Local Density-Based Score Normalization in Anomalous Sound Detection
papers.titleJa: ギャップに注意: 異常音検出におけるローカル密度基準スコア正規化のためのクラスタ退出検出
papers.what: 異常音検出におけるローカル密度基準のスコア正規化の性能を向上させるため、クラスタ退出検出という軽量なメカニズムを提案。
papers.novel: 近傍サイズを事前に固定するのではなく、ローカリティを保持するように動的に調整するクラスタ退出検出を導入した点が新規性。
papers.method: 距離の不連続性を検出することで適切な近傍サイズを選択し、ローカル密度推定の精度を向上させる仕組みを提案。
papers.validation: 複数の埋め込みモデルとデータセットを用いた実験で、近傍サイズ選択に対するロバスト性と性能向上を実証。
papers.discussion: 提案手法が他の異常検出タスクにどの程度適用可能か、さらなる検証が必要。

nextReads.label: Local Density-Based Methods for Anomaly Detection (2020)
Dynamic Neighborhood Selection for Anomaly Detection (2022)

papers.abstract: Local density-based score normalization is an effective component of distance-based embedding methods for anomalous sound detection, particularly when data densities vary across conditions or domains. In practice, however, performance depends strongly on neighborhood size. Increasing it can degrade detection accuracy when neighborhood expansion crosses cluster boundaries, violating the locality assumption of local density estimation. This observation motivates adapting the neighborhood size based on locality preservation rather than fixing it in advance. We realize this by proposing cluster exit detection, a lightweight mechanism that identifies distance discontinuities and selects neighborhood sizes accordingly. Experiments across multiple embedding models and datasets show improved robustness to neighborhood-size selection and consistent performance gains.

trend: ① スペクトル・空間特徴の統合処理や音声信号と自然言語を結びつける手法など、音声分離や音声-テキスト検索の新しいアプローチが提案されている。
② 音声言語モデルにおける公平性、安全性、説明可能性を向上させるための評価フレームワークや新手法が注目されている。
③ 継続的マルチタスク学習や半教師あり学習を活用し、ユニバーサル音声表現や長時間音声認識の精度向上を目指す研究が進展している。
